title: Spotify Expands Prompted Playlists to Podcasts
summary: Spotify's Prompted Playlists now includes podcasts, helping Premium users discover new shows. The feature uses text prompts to generate customized playlists.
body:
Spotify has expanded its Prompted Playlists feature to include podcasts, making it easier for Premium users to find new shows to listen to. Originally launched as a beta feature in December for music, the update now allows users to generate customized playlists of podcast episodes using text prompts. This feature enables users to steer the algorithm towards specific genres or themes, creating a more personalized listening experience.

 The expansion of Prompted Playlists to podcasts is significant, as it provides users with a more discovery-driven approach to finding new content. By using text prompts, users can explore different topics and genres, potentially leading to the discovery of new favorite podcasts. The feature's ability to generate customized playlists also saves users time and effort in searching for new content.

 As the feature is still in beta, it will be interesting to see how Spotify continues to develop and refine Prompted Playlists for podcasts. The company's decision to expand the feature to podcasts demonstrates its commitment to providing users with a more personalized and engaging listening experience. With this update, Spotify Premium users can look forward to discovering new podcasts and enjoying a more tailored listening experience.

With Prompted Playlists, you can use text prompts to generate customized playlists of podcast episodes. Say goodbye to endless scrolling!
The expansion of Prompted Playlists to podcasts is a game-changer for discovery. What new shows will you find?
Spotify's commitment to personalization is clear. What do you think about the update? Share your thoughts!
